companies.name: Vale S.A.
companies.dataFreshness: Investment Memo V1 2026-05-31 + Portfolio Dashboard V2.3 static QA 2026-06-21

triggers.t: Iron ore price, China steel demand and property cycle direction versus base-case 95–100 USD/t iron ore assumption
C1 cash cost normalization toward 2026 guidance range and iron ore all-in cost trend
Base Metals EBITDA contribution, expanded net debt direction and shareholder return flexibility

caveats.text: Valuation is attractive, but not sufficient alone for aggressive add because China, iron ore, Brazil, ESG, Simandou and expanded net debt risks cap the acceptable multiple
C1 cash cost was above the 2026 guidance range in 1Q26; failure to normalize would weaken the case
Expanded net debt around 17.8bn USD limits capital return flexibility if iron ore weakens

companies.next: Q2/Q3 2026 checkpoint: iron ore price and China demand, C1 cost normalization, Base Metals EBITDA contribution, expanded net debt and shareholder return flexibility

companies.name: Golar LNG Limited
companies.action: OWN · ADD ONLY ON CLEAR WEAKNESS
companies.dataFreshness: Investment Memo V1 2026-06-01 + Portfolio Dashboard V2.3 static QA 2026-06-21

triggers.t: San Matias pipeline / feedgas evacuation progress and Hilli Argentina redeployment milestones
MKII asset-level financing terms, conversion schedule and capex discipline
LNG price / commodity-upside environment sustaining FOB 10-12 or better while Gimi overproduction evidence repeats

caveats.text: Argentina/SESA/San Matias is a dominant concentration risk, not a generic country-risk footnote
MKII is the largest forward EBITDA driver, but also a major execution and financing risk
Commodity upside is real but scenario-weighted; FOB 15, FLNG #4 and strategic review are options, not base-case valuation anchors

companies.next: Q2/Q3 2026 checkpoint: San Matias pipeline/feedgas progress, MKII financing and conversion execution, Hilli redeployment, Gimi repeat overproduction evidence and LNG commodity-upside environment
